title: The ultimate walk around Boston debuts this weekend, and it’s 91 miles long
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Boston Boundless Trail, a 91-mile urban hiking loop, debuts this weekend, tracing Boston's perimeter through its neighborhoods.
The trail, created by Walking City Trails, curates existing paths without new infrastructure, beginning in North End and ending in East Boston.
The trail aims to encourage exploration and address historic segregation, promoting social cross-pollination and discovering Boston's connected green spaces.

title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a Greater Boston news quiz to test residents' knowledge of local events.
The quiz covers recent trending stories in the Greater Boston area as of June 2026.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.

title: New Hampshire man arrested in 1993 homicide of Maine woman
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Daniel Jolly, 59, was arrested in New Hampshire for the 1993 murder of Maxine Bitomski in Kittery, Maine.
Jolly worked for a medical supply company that provided services to Bitomski prior to her January 1993 death.
Maine State Police reopened the case in 2021, leading to Jolly's arrest after ongoing investigations.

title: Red Sox demote Brayan Bello after another disastrous start
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox demoted pitcher Brayan Bello to Triple-A Worcester following another poor start against the Orioles.
Bello has struggled with a 16.88 ERA in the first inning this season, impacting team performance.
Despite a strong 2025 season, Bello's recent starts led to his optioning to the minors for adjustments.

title: Mass. police department decries dangerous behavior on e-bikes after 3 youth charged
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Since November 2024, Attleboro police have received 143 reports of teens riding e-bikes recklessly.
A 16-year-old was charged with multiple offenses after provoking an officer with dangerous e-bike riding.
Two other juveniles were arrested for attempting to steal the e-bike from police storage.

title: Keep a look out for the Northern Lights over New England skies Thursday into Friday
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The aurora borealis is expected to be visible over New England late Thursday into Friday morning, according to NOAA.
Peak viewing hours for the Northern Lights are projected between 10 p.m. and 2 a.m., weather permitting.
Skywatchers are encouraged to avoid light pollution and use cameras to capture the aurora's colors.

title: Am I required to take my new vehicle to the dealer for maintenance?
author: John Paul
bullets: Routine maintenance on leased vehicles, like the 2026 Subaru Outback, need not be done at a dealer to maintain warranty.
Non-dealer services should use correct oil specifications and quality filters; document service details for warranty coverage.
Subaru vehicles may require specific synthetic oils; confirm service providers use Subaru-compliant oil standards.

title: Worcester man gets 4 to 7 years in prison for killing neighbor; family calls sentence a ‘slap in the face’
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Enrique Canales-Martinez sentenced to 4-7 years for manslaughter in the death of neighbor Jason Mayhew in Worcester.
The argument over loud music escalated, resulting in Mayhew being shot twice by Canales-Martinez on June 21, 2025.
Mayhew’s family criticized the sentence, describing it as a 'slap in the face' following the court's decision.

title: Holyoke church cancels service, temporarily closes after theft
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: St. Paul's Episcopal Church in Holyoke canceled services and closed after a theft targeting their fundraiser items.
Stolen items include food and raffle prizes for an annual golf tournament, supporting church renovations and daycare.
Holyoke police were contacted about suspicious activity; new security measures are being implemented at the church.

title: NFL insider: ‘Good chance’ Patriots will sign Christian Gonzalez to huge contract extension
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Christian Gonzalez could sign a major contract extension with the Patriots this offseason, according to NFL insider Ian Rapoport.
Gonzalez is set to earn $2.2 million this season, with his salary jumping to $18.1 million under a fifth-year option in 2027.
Gonzalez's absence from voluntary OTAs may signal leverage tactics; Coach Vrabel expects him back for mandatory minicamp next week.
